policy.canonical_pattern: active root owners use codex-<domain>; child capabilities use codex-<parent>-<capability> and live under the parent subskills directory
decision_basis: actual trigger, workflow, artifacts, safety boundary, and owning parent
required_updates: module registry, architecture, requirements, workflows, and user-facing documentation
all executable paths, tests, and global discovery interfaces
compatibility entry and migration record when a live name changes
parent SKILL routing and the canonical subskill path
policy.compatibility: Keep the historical top-level entry for at least one release, route new work to the canonical name, and remove it only after no live references remain.

conditions: the owner contract, trigger, artifacts, knowledge, and safety boundary remain unchanged
actual contract, user-recognizable language, and verified experience all favor the new name
the migration record, one-release compatibility alias, reference updates, and validation gates are complete
exclusions: add, merge, split, deprecate, delete, or materially revise an owner
change runtime, credential, installation, publication, or safety authority

migrations.reason: The capability performs provider routing and diagnostics under credential management; the historical name implies a provider selection rather than its actual capability.
The interim root name is clear but does not express its credential-management owner.
Data-faithful figure optimization is a specialized image-workflow execution mode, not an independent owner.
